name: Tianwen-1
first: First mission to deliver orbiter, lander, and rover to Mars in a single launch
description: China's first Mars mission accomplished what no agency had ever attempted in one launch: orbital insertion, atmospheric entry, soft landing, and rover deployment. The Zhurong rover operated 358 sols at Utopia Planitia, traversing 1.9 km, before entering planned hibernation in May 2022; it has not woken since (likely due to dust accumulation on solar panels). The orbiter remains active, mapping the planet at high resolution and serving as relay for ground assets.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Long March 5 from Wenchang — China's first super-heavy interplanetary launch.

events.label: MARS ORBIT INSERTION
events.note: Engine burn captures spacecraft into Mars orbit.

events.label: ZHURONG LANDING
events.note: Lander/rover descends to Utopia Planitia 2021-05-14; Zhurong rolls off ramp 2021-05-22.

events.label: ZHURONG HIBERNATION
events.note: Rover enters planned dormancy 2022-05; orbiter continues operations.
